message:
fix(ci/coverage): bump RUST_MIN_STACK to 16 MiB for llvm-cov

With `-D warnings` removed (previous commit), the Coverage job now
compiles cleanly and actually executes the test binary. One test —
`pages_cli_flag_tests::include_attachments_still_accepted_when_explicitly_passed`
— overflows the default 2 MiB thread stack when run under llvm-cov
instrumentation, because clap's derive-generated parse path has deep
nested stack frames that get inlined + instrumented on top of each
other (see Coverage run 24531525444 log: "thread ... has overflowed
its stack / fatal runtime error: stack overflow, aborting").

Setting `RUST_MIN_STACK=16777216` is the standard workaround for
llvm-cov-induced stack pressure; it has no effect on production runs
and matches what many downstream crates do in their coverage pipelines.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.7 (1M context) <noreply@anthropic.com>

files.patch:
@@ -78,6 +78,11 @@ jobs:
           # Deterministic test execution
           RUST_TEST_THREADS: 1
           PROPTEST_CASES: 32  # Fixed seed count for property tests
+          # llvm-cov instrumentation balloons stack frames enough to overflow
+          # the default 2 MiB thread stack inside clap derive parsers
+          # (pages_cli_flag_tests). Bump to 16 MiB to match what cargo test
+          # uses natively for deeply nested generated code.
+          RUST_MIN_STACK: "16777216"
         run: |
           # Run with deterministic options and skip known flaky tests
           cargo llvm-cov --workspace --lib -j 1 \
